sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The movable object didn't move - it's still at cols 20-21. But the charcoal node at the top (Node 1) changed from 18 to 19 pixels, and its boundary shifted from [[0, 46], [0, 63]] to [[0, 45], [0, 63]]. This is the timer/progress bar at the top. So clicking the uppermost blue block just affected the timer, not the movable object.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. Maybe the blue blocks control different things:
- Blue at rows 16-19 (above first bar): affects timer
- Blue at rows 24-27 (between bars): affects something else
- Blue at rows 36-39 (above second bar): moves object LEFT
- Blue at rows 44-47 (below second bar): moves object RIGHT
Wait, I need to rethink this. The object is at cols 20-21, target is at cols 28-29. I can't move it further right with the current blue block. Maybe I need to use a different blue block to move it further.
Let me try the blue block at rows 24-27 (between the two bars) to see what it does.

sections.label: ASSISTANT

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 1}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
